meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage SaaS pricing models to overcome a lack of portfolio. An advanced guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid development.
content.answer_block: Transform your lack of a portfolio into a competitive advantage by building a 'Pricing Intelligence Dashboard' for clients. By automating complex SaaS tiered-pricing logic, you demonstrate high-level business logic expertise, proving your value to stakeholders immediately while creating a repeatable, high-ticket asset that solves a universal revenue problem.
content.intro:
The blank slate is a developer's greatest enemy. You have the skills to build, but without a portfolio, you are invisible to high-paying clients who demand proof of business acumen, not just code. The silence of an empty GitHub profile is deafening, and every day without a case study is a missed opportunity for six-figure contracts.

But what if you didn't need a client to build a portfolio piece? By engineering a sophisticated SaaS pricing engine, you shift the narrative from 'I can code' to 'I can grow your revenue.' This isn't just a project; it's a strategic asset that proves your worth to the most discerning stakeholders in the tech space.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Tier Logic Engine
steps.description: Structure the data schema for multi-tiered pricing, including feature gating and usage-based billing logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing engine that supports monthly/yearly billing, feature-flag based access control, and usage-based metering.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize project structure with Zod validation for pricing tiers'

steps.title: Build the Revenue Visualization Component
steps.description: Implement a dashboard that visualizes MRR growth based on different pricing scenarios to show business impact.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React component using Recharts that visualizes projected MRR growth based on tiered pricing conversion rates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Add Recharts integration and mock data generator for pricing projections'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe-Ready API Endpoints
steps.description: Develop backend logic that mirrors production-grade Stripe integration requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an Express.js controller that calculates pro-rated upgrades and downgrades for subscription billing cycles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create backend route handlers for subscription logic and Stripe webhook simulation'

steps.title: Implement Client-Facing Pricing Calculator
steps.description: Build an interactive UI that allows users to toggle features and see real-time price updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Construct a dynamic pricing table component that updates totals in real-time based on toggle state and usage limits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Refactor pricing UI for state management and real-time calculation efficiency'

steps.title: Deploy and Document as a Case Study
steps.description: Package the project as a high-conversion portfolio asset with professional documentation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md that highlights the business problem, the technical solution, and the ROI of the pricing engine.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate README and automated test suite for deployment readiness'

pro_tips: Focus on edge-case handling like mid-cycle churn and plan migration; clients care more about how you handle failure than success.
Use mock API latency in your demo to force the implementation of professional loading states—it shows you build for real-world UX.
Always include a 'Cost-to-Build vs. Revenue-Generated' analysis in your documentation; stakeholders speak in ROI, not lines of code.
Modularize your pricing engine so it can be dropped into any future client project as a white-label module.
Add a 'dark mode' toggle and accessibility audit reports to your repo—most developers skip this, and it signals senior-level attention to detail.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, record a 60-second Loom video walking through the business logic of your pricing engine and pin it to your LinkedIn featured section.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect client to hand you a project. Build the proof you need to command top-tier rates today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
